other_observers: none; bird was seen and reported by others in the following days, Including Bob Brown and Larry Modesitt
description: A phalarope based on size, shape and behavior in mostly basic plumage. Plain (unstreaked) pale gray mantle, white bellow with white belly, breast, throat and face. Face with a black trianglular mark behind the eye. Gray mantle color extended up the back of the neck, nape and cown. Forehead was white. Bill was short and thick, relative to RNPH. Head and neck were thick.Behaviors: Swimming in circular patterns while picking up morsels from the water. Stayed mostly in the middle of the lake. No other phalaropes were present at the time of this observation.Call: nonePlumage: basic
photographer: Photographer: Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek
